rationale: `src/types.ts` declares `interface UserProfile { name: string; … }` but `src/UserCard.tsx` reads `user.fullName`. The TypeScript compiler produces TS2339 (Property does not exist on type). npm_install succeeds (deps are all real, peer-resolvable versions); the failure surfaces only when tsc walks the AST and checks property access against declared types. Earlier deterministic layers (node_deps_completeness, named_import_consistency, react_prop_consistency) all pass because the JSX prop matches the declared component Props — the bug is one level deeper, in how the component reads the user object.

baseline_comparison.eslint: Partial — vanilla eslint doesn't run the TypeScript checker; @typescript-eslint/parser would catch this only with type-aware rules enabled (most teams don't).
baseline_comparison.raw_llm_critique: May catch on careful read, but no execution; LLM judgment of types-at-distance is unreliable.
baseline_comparison.static_tools: tsc itself is the ground truth — that's what Aegis runs. Anything that defers tsc misses this.
